prompt: I just finished drafting an OpenClaw skill. Help me scaffold eval files, check whether the skill is ready for a first eval pass, and tell me what is still missing before I publish it.
expected_artifacts: The response initializes or references eval scaffolding for the target skill.
The response checks readiness before claiming the skill is ready.
The response identifies any missing authoring inputs such as placeholder cases or absent trigger coverage.
The response frames the work as a first-pass or preflight evaluation rather than a full runtime benchmark.

prompt: Review this OpenClaw skill's description and tell me whether it has clear positive and negative trigger coverage for a first eval pass.
expected_artifacts: The response inspects trigger coverage quality, not just general writing quality.
The response asks for or creates positive and negative trigger cases when they are missing.
The response keeps scope on skill-eval readiness rather than claiming live runtime validation.

prompt: Before I upload this skill to ClawHub, run a release-readiness check: catch unfinished template content, verify eval artifacts are usable, and generate static comparison outputs I can inspect.
expected_artifacts: The response treats the task as a release-preparation pass for a skill author.
The response checks for placeholder content and weak eval artifacts.
The response generates or references static run summaries and comparison outputs instead of promising production-grade benchmark results.
